title: Coffee and peppermint scalp scrub
subtitle: A ground-coffee and coconut-oil scrub for removing product build-up from the scalp
excerpt: Ground coffee exfoliates dead skin cells and product residue from the scalp; coconut oil conditions; peppermint essential oil stimulates circulation and leaves a cooling, clean sensation. Use once a fortnight on dry or build-up-prone scalps.
sourceNotes: Physical exfoliation and scalp health: Schwartz et al., Skin Pharmacology and Physiology (2008). Peppermint oil and dermal blood flow: Göbel et al., Cephalalgia (1994). Coffee-ground exfoliant properties: standard cosmetic formulation reference.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix fresh for each use; the oil and grounds separate on storage and the coconut oil can go rancid with the coffee grounds once mixed.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Mix fresh and use in one treatment. Discard any remainder.

glossaryTerms.definition: Finely ground roasted coffee for use as a physical exfoliant. Spent (used) grounds from a cafetiere work equally well -- the oils have leached out but the cellulose structure remains intact as an abrasive.

content.text: as a physical exfoliant to break up product build-up and flakes on the scalp, bound in coconut oil which conditions the hair as it works. Peppermint essential oil adds a strong cooling sensation and increases blood flow to the scalp. Spent cafetiere grounds work as well as fresh ground coffee -- the exfoliant value is in the physical structure, not the caffeine.

items.prepNote: about 30 g; spent cafetiere grounds also work
about 0.4 ml; do not exceed 10 drops as peppermint is strong
about 0.25 ml; omit during first trimester

content.text: Melt the coconut oil if it is solid. Combine with the coffee grounds and stir to coat. Add the peppermint and rosemary essential oils and mix through. The scrub should have the consistency of a thick, gritty paste.

Apply to a dry or damp scalp in sections, working the paste in with the fingertips in small circular movements. Focus on the areas where build-up is heaviest -- typically the parting and around the hairline. Leave on for 5 minutes to let the peppermint work, then rinse out thoroughly with warm water before shampooing.
Coffee grounds block drains. Place a small strainer over the plughole or clean the shower drain immediately after rinsing the scrub out.

Use once a fortnight for general scalp maintenance. For oily, product-heavy scalps, once a week is fine. Follow immediately with shampoo to remove the oil and coffee grounds, then condition as normal.
